debug_info.goal:
k : ℕ
hk : 0 < k
S : Finset ℕ
hS : ∀ p ∈ S, Odd p ∧ Nat.Prime p
p₁ p₂ : Fin S.card ≃ ↥S
hp₁ : IMO2022P3.Condition k S p₁
hp₂ : IMO2022P3.Condition k S p₂
⊢ (∃ i, ∀ (j : Fin S.card), p₂ j = p₁ (j + i)) ∨ ∃ i, ∀ (j : Fin S.card), p₂ j = p₁ (j.rev + i)

failed_attempts:
-- Define the adjacency relation on S
  let adj (a b : ℕ) : Prop := ∃ x : ℕ, 0 < x ∧ a * b = x ^ 2 + x + k
  
  -- Lemma: For any a ∈ S, there are at most 2 neighbors b ∈ S with b < a.
  have h_degree : ∀ a ∈ S, (S.filter (fun b => b < a ∧ adj a b)).card ≤ 2 := by
    intro a ha
    -- If there are no such neighbors, card is 0 ≤ 2.
    -- The condition ab = x^2+x+k implies (2x+1)^2 = 4ab - (4k-1).
    -- Let D = 4k-1. Then (2x+1)^2 ≡ -D [mod 4a].
    -- We count b < a by counting possible x.
    -- Since b < a, 4ab < 4a^2, so (2x+1)^2 < 4a^2 - D < 4a^2.
    -- Hence 0 < 2x+1 < 2a.
    -- The number of solutions to y^2 ≡ -D [mod 4a] in (0, 2a) is restricted.
    -- Since a is an odd prime, (ZZ/4aZZ)* ≅ (ZZ/4ZZ)* × (ZZ/aZZ)*.
    -- Squares mod 4 are 0, 1. -D = 1-4k ≡ 1 [mod 4]. So y^2 ≡ 1 mod 4 implies y odd.
    -- Modulo a, y^2 ≡ -D has at most 2 solutions in [0, a).
    -- Lifting to mod 4a (with y odd) preserves the count.
    -- So at most 2 values of x, hence at most 2 values of b.
    sorry -- The number theory details are standard but lengthy to write out fully.

  -- The set of edges in a cycle p is {(p i, p (i+1))}.
  let edges (p : Fin S.card ≃ S) : Finset (Sym2 ℕ) :=
    (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin S.card)).map 
      ⟨fun i => Sym2.mk (p i) (p (i + 1)), 
       fun i j h => by sorry⟩ -- injectivity of edges map for cycles

  -- We claim the set of edges is unique for any valid cycle.
  -- Strategy: "Peel" the graph from largest to smallest vertex.
  -- Let m = max S. In any cycle, m has degree 2.
  -- Neighbors of m must be < m. There are at most 2 such valid neighbors.
  -- So m MUST connect to exactly those 2.
  -- This fixes the edges incident to m.
  -- Inductively, the edges incident to any v are determined by 
  -- the "required" degree (2 minus connections to larger nodes) and available smaller neighbors.
  
  have h_edges_eq : edges p₁ = edges p₂ := by
    sorry -- Follows from h_degree and the existence of the cycle.

  -- If two cycles on the same set have the same edges, one is a rotation/reflection of the other.
  have h_geom : (∃ i, ∀ j, p₂ j = p₁ (j + i)) ∨ ∃ i, ∀ j, p₂ j = p₁ (Fin.rev j + i) := by
    sorry -- Combinatorial fact about Hamiltonian cycles.

  exact h_geom
